workflow: Compile and independently verify performance-critical pure Common Lisp kernels under SBCL.
rationale: The existing compile-pure-lisp skill already owns the complete specialization and verification workflow. The public repository improves distribution, examples, tests, and evidence without creating a competing skill trigger.
limits: Lexical trigger similarity is a candidate signal, not proof of semantic overlap.
Word counts describe instruction volume, not measured model latency.
The catalog observation does not prove future workflow frequency.
